blurb:
Topgolf is less about the food and more about having a good time, and it delivers on that front. The bays are fun whether you golf or not, and the drinks and apps keep things going. It works great for a group outing, a corporate thing, or a low-key date where you want something to actually do.

The food is solid bar food. Think burgers, shareables, and drinks that are easy to order another round of. Nobody is coming here for a quiet dinner, but that's not the point.

They're open late every night, until 1am most days, so it's a good option when you want to keep the night going. Book a bay ahead of time on weekends, because waits can be long if you just walk in.
address: 1600 Ellsworth Industrial Blvd NW, Atlanta, GA 30318, USA
hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
OK Cafe is a classic Atlanta diner that has been doing the same thing for decades and doing it well. It's the kind of place that has a jukebox, real milkshakes, and meatloaf on the menu. Comfortable, unpretentious, and genuinely good Southern comfort food.

The meatloaf is the thing people love here. The milkshakes are thick and old-school. If you want breakfast, they open at 7am every day of the week, which makes it a great option on a Saturday morning when you don't want to cook.

Prices are reasonable, the portions are generous, and you don't need a reservation. It's a good pick for families, weekend breakfast, or any time you want a no-fuss meal.
address: 1284 W Paces Ferry Rd NW, Atlanta, GA 30327, USA
hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Yeppa is one of those places that looks as good as it tastes, which is saying something. The interior is genuinely beautiful, the cocktails are creative and strong, and the whole vibe works for a date night or a girls' night out. It's lively without being too loud to have a conversation.

The tuna appetizer gets mentioned constantly in reviews, and for good reason. The pasta is the other thing people keep coming back for. And if they still have the ice cream with olive oil and sea salt on the menu, order it. It sounds weird and it's not.

It stays open until midnight on Fridays and Saturdays, so it also works as a late dinner after something else in Buckhead.
address: 306 Buckhead Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30305, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
If you want a special-occasion dinner that actually feels special, Delbar in Buckhead delivers. The place is buzzing on a Friday night in the best way, and the open kitchen adds to the energy. This is a great pick for an anniversary, a birthday, or any time you want the night to feel like an event.

The Middle Eastern flavors are bold and well done. Reviews consistently call out the food as a step above what you'd expect from the Buckhead strip. It's the kind of place where you'll want to order a few things and share them across the table.

They're open for lunch too, Monday through Friday starting at 11:30am, which makes it a solid option if you want to impress someone without waiting for dinner. Weekends they open at 11am.
address: 3060 Peachtree Rd Suite R-160, Atlanta, GA 30305, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Canoe is the one you save for the nights that actually matter. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, the garden setting is genuinely pretty, and the New American food is farm-to-table done right. My wife and I have been twice for anniversaries and it has never let us down.

The menu rotates with the seasons, so what you get depends on when you go. But the kitchen consistently earns its reputation. This is the kind of place where the whole experience, the setting, the service, the food, comes together the way it should.

Just plan ahead. They close between lunch and dinner Tuesday through Friday, and they're on the pricier side. Saturday is dinner only, and Sunday brunch is worth the drive if you've never been.
address: 4199 Paces Ferry Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
4Thirteen is a solid sports bar that actually takes the food seriously, which is not always a given. Good spot for watching a game, grabbing drinks with friends, or just a laid-back night out when you don't want anything fancy. The atmosphere is lively without being overwhelming.

The catfish basket gets high marks, and the mac and cheese and candied yams are the kind of sides that make you reconsider every sports bar you've been to before. The hot honey wing basket is another crowd favorite and a good call if you want something with a little kick.

Keep in mind they're closed Tuesdays, and the kitchen doesn't open until 4pm on weekdays. Saturday and Sunday they get going at 1pm, which is perfect for afternoon game days.
address: 755 State Hwy 121 BYP, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 1:00 PM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 1:00 PM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
Manna is the kind of place you bring someone when you want dinner to be an actual event. Korean BBQ and hot pot at the same spot, with grills right at the table and a staff that genuinely walks you through the whole thing if it's your first time. It's interactive, it's fun, and it's great for a group.

First-timers should know the servers here are a big part of what makes the experience. People rave about how patient and attentive the staff is, explaining exactly how to cook everything so you don't end up with overcooked meat. It takes a little of the guesswork out and makes the whole meal more enjoyable.

With a 4.8 rating across more than 4,000 reviews, this one is hard to argue with. Book ahead if you're coming on a weekend.
address: 2405 S Stemmons Fwy #300, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Delhi6 is the kind of Indian restaurant that works for just about any occasion, whether it's a weeknight dinner, a date night, or getting a group together. With over 4,000 reviews and a 4.6 rating, this place has clearly built a loyal following in Highland Village for good reason. The menu covers traditional curries and dishes from different regions of India, so there's usually something for everyone at the table.

The bar side of things makes it a solid option if you want to linger a bit after dinner. It's a step above your typical strip mall Indian spot in terms of atmosphere.

They're closed Mondays, and lunch service doesn't start until noon on Fridays through Sundays, so plan accordingly.
address: 2420 Justin Rd, Highland Village, TX 75077, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Luci's is one of those neighborhood spots that becomes a regular rotation without you even realizing it. It opens early at 7am every day, which makes it a good call for breakfast, lunch, or a casual dinner without much fuss. The crowd is friendly and the menu hits the comfort food notes most people are looking for.

The sopa is a go-to for a lot of regulars and it's easy to see why. It's the kind of dish that keeps people coming back on a weeknight when you just want something satisfying and familiar.

One heads up: service can slow down when it gets busy, so if you're in a rush, maybe go during off-peak hours or call ahead for takeout.
address: 1305 S State Hwy 121 #166, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
If you have a sweet tooth and haven't been to Petit Four yet, you're missing out. This little dessert cafe is the kind of place you go when you want something special after dinner or just need a treat on a Friday night. The vibe is cute and Instagram-worthy, but the desserts are the real reason to go.

The strawberry Oreo tart is what gets talked about the most, and for good reason. It's more substantial than it looks because there's a chocolate cake layer underneath. The choco banana pudding tart is another solid pick. Fair warning though: this place leans heavy on chocolate, so if that's your thing, you'll be very happy here.

Just keep in mind they're closed Mondays, and they don't open until 11am on Sundays. Worth planning around.
